design_philosophy.core_loop: Turn-based RPG combat with resource-constrained magic system requiring strategic HP management across battles
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Attrition management: magic casting depletes HP instead of a separate resource, forcing constant tension between offensive capability and survival
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Lead a mercenary company through a sci-fantasy world to stop an awakened ancient machine and a dictator's ambitions
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Radical rejection of standard MP system—magic costs HP, making healing spells dangerous (one heals but kills the caster). Bottles with limited uses and no purchasable healing items create scarcity. Mercenary recruitment system replaces traditional party building, letting players either level weak hires or swap for stronger ones.

difficulty.checkpointing: Town-based (inns restore HP/healing bottle uses; save system allows reloading)
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: High agency: players choose to level weak mercenaries for challenge or hire stronger ones to reduce difficulty; healing bottle scarcity and HP-based magic create inherent tension that cannot be eliminated through standard grinding

progression.upgrade_system: Spirit learning system: characters unlock 8 base spirits (Fire, Earth, Water, Air, Sphere, Sky, Heart, Light). Each spirit learned individually provides 1 spell; each spirit pair provides 1 additional spell; mastering all 8 unlocks 1 final spell = 37 total spells. Spirit experience increases as related spells are cast.
progression.unlock_mechanism: Story progression unlocks new mercenaries (most for hire); stronger mercenaries become available as player advances, some tied to story events; new spirits are learned through gameplay
replayability_hooks: Choice to level old mercenaries vs. hire new stronger ones creates alternative party compositions
37-spell spirit combination system allows varied magic loadouts
Mercenary roster decisions (all except one can be rehired) permit different team strategies on replays

world_building_techniques: Character-driven plot with main antagonist (Zaygos, young dictator)
Science fantasy setting (ancient machines, spirits, magic school)
Protagonist guilt narrative (Chezni awakens Dal Gren on a dare, must fix it)

notable_bosses: Dal Gren (ancient machine of immense power; central plot device)
Zaygos (young dictator antagonist seeking Dal Gren)

faction_indicators: Zaygos faction (seeks to control ancient technology for power)
Magic school authority (Headmaster ordering hero to stop Dal Gren)
danger_signals: Accidental awakening of world-threatening ancient machine (Dal Gren)
Dictator attempting to seize ancient power
Destruction of institutional knowledge centers (magic school destroyed)
environmental_storytelling: Magic school destroyed as consequence of hero's mistake
Towns providing shelter and healing resources (inns central to survival)
Overworld map showing world scale and travel necessity
NPC mercenaries joining cause suggests spreading awareness of threat
